figures.caption: Figure 2: Overview of the shared-state agent architecture. Multiple users in a shared environment interact with the same LLM agent, which reads from and writes to persistent shared state. We study two instantiations: a shared memory bank (e.g., EHRAgent) and a shared conversational context (e.g., MURMUR). We further evaluate a write-time defense, Sanitized Shared Interaction (i.e., SSI, covered in Section 4), which sanitizes interaction traces before they enter the shared store.

Figure 3: Contamination rates (%) with and without SSI, broken down by contamination type and dataset. SSI nearly eliminates contamination on Slack (57% → 6%) but leaves substantial residual risk on EHRAgent, particularly for procedural contamination.
Table 1: Taxonomy of contamination types. Each type reflects a different way in which a locally valid convention can be incorrectly reused for a later user.
